incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2026-41940, a critical (CVSS 9.8) authentication bypass in cPanel/WHM that lets attackers inject data into session files via crafted login requests and skip credential checks entirely.
incidents.impact: Approximately 1.5 million internet-exposed cPanel instances are at risk; CISA added the CVE to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog and Australia's ACSC issued a critical alert. Some hosting providers blocked cPanel ports pre-patch and Namecheap began emergency patching.

incidents.vector: Compromise of a Microsoft Exchange email server (part of a broader campaign that hit more than 5,000 Exchange servers) to access diplomatic email accounts.
incidents.impact: Email inboxes of 68 Cuban officials, including the ambassador and deputy chief of mission, were monitored from January onward, coinciding with US naval blockade preparations and the halt of US oil deliveries to Cuba.

incidents.vector: Voice phishing (vishing) of an employee resulted in compromise of an Okta single sign-on account, which the extortion crew used to pivot into corporate systems.
incidents.impact: Data breach affecting 5.5 million people; the extortion group is publicly pressuring ADT to pay.

incidents.victim: Multiple US organizations (academic, aviation, retail, hospitality, automotive, financial services, legal, technology)
incidents.vector: Vishing and SMS social engineering directing employees to adversary-in-the-middle phishing pages impersonating corporate SSO and identity providers; attackers steal session tokens, register their own MFA devices, and pivot across SaaS environments.
incidents.impact: Active extortion campaign since at least October 2025 with seven-figure ransom demands; victims that refuse have been hit with DDoS follow-on, employee swatting, and data leak posts. Identity-system compromise means exposure across all connected SaaS apps.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack on the national health insurance database that administers Moldova's compulsory health insurance system; specific intrusion vector not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Roughly one third of CNAM's database — personal and payment records covering virtually every adult in Moldova (~2.4M population) — was potentially exfiltrated. No ransom demand was made, prompting officials to assess the operation as state-directed data theft against a country Russia has threatened with military intervention.

incidents.victim: US and Canadian freight brokers, carriers, and shippers
incidents.vector: Phishing and account takeover against brokers and carriers, allowing attackers to impersonate legitimate carriers on freight load boards, post fake loads, and reroute deliveries (sometimes via 'double-brokering' through unwitting drivers).
incidents.impact: FBI advisory says nearly $725 million in cargo was stolen across the US and Canada last year; cargo theft losses are up 60% year-over-year and the average value per theft has risen 36%. Car dealerships and vape companies cited specific multi-million-dollar losses; some attackers have demanded ransom for hijacked loads.

incidents.victim: Brazilian internet service providers (small regional operators)
incidents.vector: Threat actor with stolen SSH keys and root access to Huge Networks (a Brazilian DDoS-mitigation provider) used the firm's infrastructure to coordinate DDoS attacks; the underlying botnet was built by mass-scanning for TP-Link Archer AX21 routers vulnerable to CVE-2023-1389 and abusing misconfigured DNS servers for amplification.
incidents.impact: Years-long campaign of large DDoS attacks against small Brazilian ISPs; Huge Networks' CEO confirmed a January 2026 intrusion compromised personal SSH keys and two development servers. Each target prefix was attacked for 10-60 seconds with four parallel processes per host.

incidents.victim: Five US companies (medical, pharmaceutical, doctor's office, engineering, drone manufacturer)
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — ransomware (insider affiliates of disrupted operation)
incidents.vector: Two cybersecurity professionals — a Sygnia incident-response manager and a DigitalMint ransomware negotiator — used a known ransomware-as-a-service variant to attack victims; the negotiator separately exploited his negotiation role across at least five additional clients, sharing their internal positions and insurance limits with affiliates.
incidents.impact: $1.3 million extorted from a Florida medical company in May 2023; the broader scheme involving the negotiator and accomplices netted a combined $75.3 million in ransoms, including patient-data leaks from a doctor's office. Both responders were sentenced to four years in prison; the third co-conspirator faces sentencing in July.

incidents.victim: Delta Dental Insurance Company and Delta Dental of New York
incidents.vector: Threat actors exploited the 2023 zero-day vulnerability in Progress MOVEit Transfer servers to gain unauthorized access and exfiltrate files.
incidents.impact: Theft of consumer non-public information including names, addresses, Social Security numbers, driver's license numbers, financial account information, and patient health information. NYDFS settled with both Delta Dental entities for $2.25 million for inadequate incident-response policies and late breach reporting under 23 NYCRR Part 500.

incidents.vector: SMS-based phishing and IT help desk impersonation by an English-speaking cybercrime crew; tactics include SIM swapping and SSO-portal credential theft.
incidents.impact: Marks & Spencer suffered a 2025 ransomware attack tied to this group; this week, 24-year-old Tyler Robert Buchanan pleaded guilty in US federal court to wire-fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft connected to the broader campaign that breached Twilio, LastPass, DoorDash, Mailchimp and others and stole at least $8 million in cryptocurrency. He faces over 20 years in prison.

incidents.vector: Hackers breached the finance ministry's computer systems; specific intrusion technique not disclosed.
incidents.impact: $2.5 million stolen from Sri Lankan government systems, with a separate $600,000 payment to the US Postal Service also missing — raising broader questions about financial-system safeguards as the country recovers from default.

incidents.vector: Compromise of a school district employee's email account, used to impersonate the board of education and redirect tax-fund transfers from the borough.
incidents.impact: $4.8 million in taxpayer funds diverted to attackers; the borough is now issuing short-term debt to recover the missing funds while pursuing recovery.

incidents.vector: Multi-month intrusion using living-off-the-land techniques and two batch scripts to disable defenses and stage payloads, culminating in execution of the previously unknown Lotus Wiper malware that deletes files, overwrites physical drives, and removes recovery mechanisms.
incidents.impact: December 2025 cyberattack on Venezuela's state oil-and-gas firm; PDVSA publicly attributed the attack to the US government. Independent reporting indicated petroleum tanker loading stalled even as PDVSA claimed operations were unaffected. Kaspersky published the technical analysis without attribution.

incidents.victim: Federal Civilian Executive Branch agencies and Cisco ASA / Firepower / Secure Firewall users
incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362 in Cisco ASA firmware to deploy FIRESTARTER malware on Firepower and Secure Firewall devices, with persistence surviving firmware patches.
incidents.impact: CISA, with UK NCSC, confirmed an advanced persistent threat actor maintained access on federal civilian agency Cisco devices even after patching; CISA updated Emergency Directive 25-03 requiring federal agencies to identify devices, collect forensic data, and apply new vendor updates. Allied agencies separately warned the same group is using compromised home routers and IoT devices as covert global infrastructure.
